text: The surgeon employs a dome down technique to address the lack of ICG backfill in the cystic duct during dissection, suspecting the presence of a cystic duct stone.
The surgeon has skeletonized the cystic duct, revealing a potential obstruction. Visual inspection suggests the presence of a stone, leading to the application of clips on the duct.
The surgeon transects the gallbladder just below the previously placed clip, observing for bile reflux. In the absence of bile reflux, the surgeon suspects an obstruction in the duct.

The surgeon switches instruments and visually identifies a filling defect in the duct. They gently manipulate the duct with counter traction and milk the proximal duct into the open lumen, taking care not to crush the stone.
